codeReview.summary: FrozenSpec implementation provides immutable spec reference that cannot be modified once task execution begins. The implementation correctly snapshots task state at execution start, uses Arc<FrozenSpec> for immutability guarantee, and stores specs in ExecutionController for retrieval during execution.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frozen_spec module was committed as part of merge_queue commit 3b02680. The diff shows 2 files changed in orchestrator/src/ with merge_queue.rs (1651 lines) but frozen_spec.rs was not shown in the diff - meaning the frozen_spec file was likely created separately and the commit message only describes merge_queue feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 3b02680 --stat shows only merge_queue.rs and lib.rs changes, but frozen_spec.rs exists in the codebase. This suggests the feature may have been implemented in an earlier commit.

summary: Implementation PASSES review. FrozenSpec correctly provides: (1) Snapshot of task spec on execution start via FrozenSpec::snapshot(), (2) Immutable reference during execution via Arc<FrozenSpec> wrapper in FrozenSpecRef, (3) Original spec preserved for audit via FrozenSpec struct storing description, plan, scope, and timestamps. The ExecutionController integrates frozen specs by creating them in execute_task() after start_task() transitions to Executing state, then storing in frozen_specs HashMap. Tests verify snapshot captures description, plan, scope, and that Arc provides immutability.
